senses.gloss: to give straw to, to feed with straw; to sell straw
verbal noun of ШӘЩҺШЁЩҺЩҶЩҺ (tabana, вҖңto give straw toвҖқ)
a great bowl that can satisfy the thirst of twenty, a vessel used to fill drinking cups
a generous chieftain, one who gives large amounts
a wolf for its thirst for blood being likened to a great vessel's amount
to be sagacious, to be callid, to be skillful in knowing, to be minute in inspection
intelligent, callid, sagacious, skillful in knowing, minute in inspection
verbal noun of ШӘЩҺШЁЩҗЩҶЩҺ (tabina, вҖңto be callidвҖқ)

etymology.text:
Likely borrowed from Aramaic ЧӘЦҙЦјЧ‘Ц°Ч ЦёЧҗ / Ь¬Ь’ЬўЬҗ (teбёҮnДҒ, вҖңstrawвҖқ), which is suspected mainly because of the varying vocalization, and also because this root is underdeveloped or it has the unrelated meaning of a вҖңbowlвҖқ. The Aramaic might in turn come from Akkadian р’…”р’үЎр’Ғ• (/вҒ tibnuвҒ /, вҖңstrawвҖқ) but can likewise be cognate. Else the word is only present in Hebrew ЧӘЦ¶ЦјЧ‘Ц¶Чҹ (teбёҮen, вҖңstrawвҖқ), in which language there is nothing to relate to either.

Denominal verb of ШӘЩҗШЁЩ’ЩҶ (tibn, вҖңstrawвҖқ). In the вҖңto bungвҖқ sense allegedly motivated by Spanish taponar.

Uncertain with possible connection to Akkadian р’Ӣ°р’үҢр’ҢҲ (/вҒ tabnД«tuвҒ /, вҖңan offering basket, a place to deposit tribute to a ruler or offerings before deitiesвҖқ, literally вҖңwell-arranged, well-made or constructedвҖқ) and Akkadian р’ҒҖр’ҖҖр’үЎ (/вҒ bДҒnuвҒ /, вҖңa ewer, vessel that fills up other vesselsвҖқ, literally вҖңthat which does what it was made for; a by-name for a ewer in ritual textsвҖқ), therefore distantly being connected to ШЁ ЩҶ ЩҠ (b n y).

Like the more productive root Ш· ШЁ ЩҶ (б№ӯ b n), from which there is with the like meanings Ш·ЩҺШЁЩҗЩҶЩҺ (б№ӯabina) and Ш·ЩҺШЁЩҗЩҶ (б№ӯabin).
